test.observation: Test executable runs successfully. Multiple test categories pass: pid (13 tests), exec (4 tests, 2 pre-existing failures in native exec), signal (36 tests), task lifecycle (10 tests), wait (14 tests), pgrp/session (18 tests), zombie (17 tests), process relationships (23 tests), orphan adoption (10 tests). Total 202+ tests passing. 2 pre-existing failures in exec native tests unrelated to process-table milestone.

typecheck.observation: CMake configuration and build completes successfully for both iOS Simulator and macOS targets. No compilation errors. All components (ixland-libc, ixland-system, ixland-wasm) build cleanly.

lint.observation: Linting passes. Minor formatting issues detected in 19 files (mostly existing code) but no blocking issues. Warnings for missing tools (clang-tidy, SwiftLint, ShellCheck) but these don't affect process-table code which is C code.

nonBlockingIssues.description: Doxygen comment claims 'lock-free allocation' but uses pthread_mutex_lock. Suggest updating to 'thread-safe'. Location: pid.c:45
Redundant atomic read inside mutex section. Location: pid.c:71
O(1) timing test only verifies average <10μs, doesn't prove O(1) vs O(log n). Location: test_pid.c:233
Static 252KB array allocated unconditionally at startup. Consider lazy init for memory-constrained devices. Location: pid.c:22

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update 'PID numbers - Atomic counter with reuse' to reflect the actual free list stack implementation with LIFO reuse pattern
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: architecture.md mentions atomic counter but implementation uses free list stack (pid.c). This caused minor confusion during review.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add clarification on when to use ixland-prefixed headers vs standard system headers in tests. test_zombie_process.c uses <sys/wait.h> while other tests use ixland headers.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: zombie test uses standard WIFEXITED macros from sys/wait.h to verify Linux compatibility, which is appropriate but not documented as a pattern.
